message:
fix(scripts): add placeholder fallbacks and detection for unconfigured hooks

When the MCP server isn't running during integration setup, the scripts now:
- Use "YOUR_AGENT_NAME" placeholder instead of empty string
- Warn users that hooks will silently skip until properly configured
- Instruct users to re-run integration after starting the server

Shell hooks (check_inbox.sh, codex_notify.sh) now detect common placeholder
patterns and silently exit instead of making failed API calls:
- YOUR_* patterns
- PLACEHOLDER
- CONFIGURE patterns

This prevents confusing error messages when users haven't completed setup.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -35,6 +35,15 @@ if [[ -z "${PROJECT}" || -z "${AGENT}" ]]; then
   exit 0
 fi
 
+# Detect placeholder values (indicates unconfigured settings)
+if [[ "${PROJECT}" == *"YOUR_"* || "${PROJECT}" == *"PLACEHOLDER"* || "${PROJECT}" == *"CONFIGURE"* ]]; then
+  # Silent exit - configuration not complete
+  exit 0
+fi
+if [[ "${AGENT}" == *"YOUR_"* || "${AGENT}" == *"PLACEHOLDER"* || "${AGENT}" == *"CONFIGURE"* ]]; then
+  exit 0
+fi
+
 # Rate limiting using temp file
 RATE_FILE="/tmp/mcp-mail-check-${AGENT//[^a-zA-Z0-9]/_}"
 NOW=$(date +%s)

@@ -153,10 +153,12 @@ if [[ ${_SERVER_AVAILABLE} -eq 1 ]]; then
   fi
 fi
 
-# If we still don't have an agent name, hooks that need it will be omitted
+# If we still don't have an agent name, use placeholder that hooks will detect
 if [[ -z "${_AGENT}" ]]; then
-  log_warn "No agent name available. Agent-specific hooks will need manual configuration."
-  log_warn "After starting the server, run: uv run python -m mcp_agent_mail.cli agents list ${_PROJ_DISPLAY}"
+  _AGENT="YOUR_AGENT_NAME"
+  log_warn "No agent name available (server not running). Using placeholder '${_AGENT}'."
+  log_warn "Hooks with placeholder values will silently skip execution."
+  log_warn "After starting the server, reconfigure with: ./scripts/integrate_claude_code.sh"
 fi
 
 log_step "Writing MCP server config and hooks"

@@ -166,8 +166,10 @@ fi
 # If we still don't have an agent name, warn the user
 _PROJ_DISPLAY=$(basename "$TARGET_DIR")
 if [[ -z "${_AGENT}" ]]; then
-  log_warn "No agent name available. Agent-specific hooks will need manual configuration."
-  log_warn "After starting the server, run: uv run python -m mcp_agent_mail.cli agents list ${_PROJ_DISPLAY}"
+  _AGENT="YOUR_AGENT_NAME"
+  log_warn "No agent name available (server not running). Using placeholder '${_AGENT}'."
+  log_warn "Hooks with placeholder values will silently skip execution."
+  log_warn "After starting the server, reconfigure integration."
 fi
 
 echo

@@ -190,13 +190,15 @@ else
   fi
 fi
 
-# If we still don't have an agent name, hooks that need it will be omitted
+# If we still don't have an agent name, use placeholder that hooks will detect
 if [[ -z "${_AGENT}" ]]; then
-  log_warn "No agent name available. Agent-specific hooks will need manual configuration."
+  _AGENT="YOUR_AGENT_NAME"
   _PROJ_DISPLAY=$(basename "$TARGET_DIR")
   _PROJ="${TARGET_DIR}"
   _MCP_DIR="${ROOT_DIR}"
-  log_warn "After starting the server, run: uv run python -m mcp_agent_mail.cli agents list ${_PROJ_DISPLAY}"
+  log_warn "No agent name available (server not running). Using placeholder '${_AGENT}'."
+  log_warn "Hooks with placeholder values will silently skip execution."
+  log_warn "After starting the server, reconfigure integration."
 fi
 
 log_step "Installing inbox check hook"
